nativeRange.habitat: None — Echinodorus 'Kleiner Bar' is a hybrid cultivar (German name 'Kleiner Bär' meaning 'Little Bear') with no wild population. The parent genus Echinodorus is native to wetlands of the Americas (marshes, riverbanks, seasonally flooded lowlands).

decorPreferences: moderate to bright lighting for best red coloration

imageCandidates.notes: Product page for Kleiner Bar Sword at Modern Aquarium. Primary listing image shows the red-bronze new growth and compact rosette habit. Retail product image; copyright holder is Modern Aquarium. URL is the product page rather than a direct image link.

summary: A compact hybrid Echinodorus cultivar (German 'Kleiner Bär' = 'Little Bear') prized for red-bronze new growth that matures to green with reddish stems. Mid-sized background sword that stays smaller than Amazon Sword or Echinodorus 'Rose'; easy care, heavy root feeder, with stronger light deepening the red coloration.
careNotes: Echinodorus 'Kleiner Bar' is a HYBRID cultivar of unknown specific parentage, sold across the trade as Echinodorus sp. 'Kleiner Bar' (German: 'Kleiner Bär', 'Little Bear') with no recognised wild population. The parent genus Echinodorus is native to wetlands of the Americas (Alismataceae, order Alismatales). It is one of the more compact named Echinodorus, typically maturing at roughly 20–35 cm — smaller than the Amazon sword complex or 'Rose' sword — which makes it usable as a background plant in mid-sized tanks or as a midground specimen in larger aquaria. Lighting is moderate by default; Modern Aquarium notes that stronger light supports the deeper red coloration of new leaves, while in moderate setups foliage is typically vibrant green with attractive reddish stems. CO2 is not required (Modern Aquarium) but is beneficial for faster growth and larger leaves. Temperature tolerance follows the general Echinodorus range — 68–82 °F (20–28 °C) per Modern Aquarium. pH, gH, and kH are not specified by the primary source; values shown here are inferred from the wider Echinodorus complex (pH 5.0–7.5, gH 4–18, kH 3–12) and should be treated as conservative defaults. This is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER like every other Echinodorus: plant in a deep nutrient-rich substrate or aquarium soil, supplement with root tabs near the root zone (not directly under the crown), and keep the crown just above the substrate surface. Steady fertilisation (especially iron and micronutrients) supports the best red colour. Newly purchased plants may melt back emersed leaves before sending up the submerged form — this is normal. Avoid drastic parameter swings to minimise melt.
breedingNotes: Propagates vegetatively by runners that produce daughter plants; once each plantlet has a small rosette and visible roots it can be cut from the runner and replanted. Like other Echinodorus, mature specimens can also produce adventitious plantlets on long emergent flower stalks and may be divided at the crown. As a hybrid cultivar, seed propagation is not commercially used; runner propagation is the standard method.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium — Kleiner Bar (Echinodorus sp. Kleiner Bar)
primary.notes: Manifest-specified primary source. Confirmed temperature 68–82 °F (20–28 °C), moderate lighting (stronger light supports deeper red coloration), CO2 not required but beneficial for faster growth, moderate growth rate, background placement, heavy root feeder needing nutrient-rich substrate and root tabs, steady fertilisation for optimal colour, easy care level, common name 'Kleiner Bar Sword', new growth develops deeper red coloration under strong light (vibrant green with reddish stems under moderate conditions). pH, gH, kH, exact adult size, and tank size not specified in source.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Echinodorus
additional.notes: Used for genus-level taxonomy (family Alismataceae, order Alismatales) and confirmation that Echinodorus species are 'native to the Americas' as wetland marsh/bog plants that grow submersed, and that species 'can hybridise in the wild or through artificial means' with 'many forms... given subspecific status or as named forms in the aquarium trade' — supporting treatment of 'Kleiner Bar' as a trade cultivar without a single wild origin.
